title: UK Low Carbon Hydrogen Standard v4 (January 2026) Updates Methodology and Compliance Rules
content: The UK Government has released Version 4 of the UK Low Carbon Hydrogen Standard (LCHS), effective January 2026, updating the greenhouse gas (GHG) emissions calculation methodology and compliance requirements for low carbon hydrogen. Key changes include simplified classification rules for Refinery Off Gas, new provisions allowing biomethane to be sourced from the gas grid, and a revised process for evidencing renewable energy use via Renewable Energy Guarantees of Origin (REGOs). The standard also expands permissible end-uses for solid carbon by-products to include asphalt and introduces a new section on stored feedstocks and fuels. Retaining the strict GHG emission intensity threshold of 20gCO2e/MJLHV, the document outlines eligible production pathways such as electrolysis and fossil gas reforming with CCS, while establishing a rigorous Monitoring, Reporting, and Verification (MRV) framework that requires monthly reporting of discrete consignments.
author: Analysts Account
